questions.question: On what date did the city of Paris witness the storming of the Bastille?
questions.answer: The Bastille was stormed on the morning of 14 July 1789. [cite: 36, 41]

questions.question: Who was the King of France when the revolution broke out?
questions.answer: Louis XVI of the Bourbon family was the King of France. [cite: 60]

questions.question: Which estate in French society was the only one that paid taxes?
questions.answer: Only the members of the third estate paid taxes. [cite: 70, 83]

questions.question: What was the 'taille'?
questions.answer: The taille was a direct tax to be paid directly to the state. [cite: 82, 98]

questions.question: What were 'tithes'?
questions.answer: Tithes were a tax levied by the church, comprising one-tenth of the agricultural produce. [cite: 81, 97]

questions.question: Define 'Subsistence crisis'.
questions.answer: It is an extreme situation where the basic means of livelihood are endangered. [cite: 128, 130]

questions.question: Who were the 'Active citizens' according to the Constitution of 1791?
questions.answer: Men above 25 years of age who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer's wage. [cite: 311]

questions.question: Which philosopher proposed a division of power within the government in 'The Spirit of the Laws'?
questions.answer: Montesquieu proposed the division of power between the legislative, executive, and judiciary. [cite: 157]

questions.question: What did the 'Broken chain' symbolise?
questions.answer: Chains were used to fetter slaves, so a broken chain stands for the act of becoming free. [cite: 377]

questions.question: Who wrote the influential pamphlet 'What is the Third Estate'?
questions.answer: Abbé Sieyès wrote the pamphlet. [cite: 231]

questions.question: What was the 'Estates General'?
questions.answer: The Estates General was a political body to which the three estates sent their representatives. [cite: 189]

questions.question: When did the National Assembly take the Tennis Court Oath?
questions.answer: They assembled and took the oath on 20 June 1789. [cite: 204, 205]

questions.question: What was the 'Marseillaise'?
questions.answer: It was a patriotic song composed by Roget de L'Isle, which is now the national anthem of France. [cite: 411, 413]

questions.question: Who were the 'sans-culottes'?
questions.answer: Jacobin members who wore long striped trousers to set themselves apart from nobles who wore knee breeches. [cite: 425, 444]

questions.question: What was the 'guillotine'?
questions.answer: A device consisting of two poles and a blade with which a person is beheaded. [cite: 462]

questions.question: Which period is referred to as the 'Reign of Terror'?
questions.answer: The period from 1793 to 1794. [cite: 460]

questions.question: When did French women finally win the right to vote?
questions.answer: Women in France won the right to vote in 1946. [cite: 549]

questions.question: Who was Olympe de Gouges?
questions.answer: One of the most important politically active women in revolutionary France who wrote the Declaration of the Rights of Woman and Citizen. [cite: 552, 553, 555]

questions.question: In which year was slavery finally abolished in French colonies?
questions.answer: Slavery was finally abolished in French colonies in 1848. [cite: 609]

questions.question: What does the 'Red Phrygian cap' symbolise?
questions.answer: It was a cap worn by a slave upon becoming free. [cite: 384, 445]

questions.question: When did Napoleon Bonaparte crown himself Emperor of France?
questions.answer: Napoleon crowned himself Emperor in 1804. [cite: 217, 650]

questions.question: At which battle was Napoleon finally defeated?
questions.answer: He was finally defeated at Waterloo in 1815. [cite: 220, 654]

questions.question: What were the 'natural and inalienable rights' mentioned in the Constitution?
questions.answer: Rights such as the right to life, freedom of speech, freedom of opinion, and equality before law. [cite: 334]

questions.question: Name the two Indian individuals who responded to the ideas coming from revolutionary France.
questions.answer: Tipu Sultan and Rammohan Roy. [cite: 664]

questions.question: What was the 'Convention'?
questions.answer: The newly elected assembly which abolished the monarchy and declared France a republic on 21 September 1792. [cite: 450]
